CONTINUE ED

WILL NOT BRING


MILITIA HERE

pages.raw_text:
They came to the conclusion that
every man who was thus starving
himself was wasting his vitality
and could not cause the city to suf-
fer financially for it. They de.
clare it is not sign of weaken
ing spirit in the fight.


LULL IN FIGHT


A decided lull is noted in the
fight today. Few men went out to
defy the police. and 1t is evident
that preparations are on foot for
a new system of attack. Leaders
are waiting for new recruits from
throughout the Pacific northwest
and middle west, for the local
ranks are thinning to such extent
that men must be taken from the
reserve force soon.


RECRUITS ON WAY


Funds and recruits are expected
from as far east as Chicago with.
in the next few days and more
men are being rushed In from
Butte to carry On the fight. The
banner calling for an uncondition
al surrender of all prisoners in the
city jail before any means of truce
shall be considered is still hanging
in conspicuous place in the hall


Not single arrest for street
speaking has been made today.


Nearly a score of government
emigration sIeuths of various ma.
tionaities and from all over the
entire Pacific northwest. Idaho
and Montana, are declared to be
in Spokane, ferreting out aliens
participating in the |. w. w. free
speech fight for the purpose Of
making a wholesale deportation
Some of the detectives are report
ed to be in jail, submitting to the
"hunger strike" in their diIieent
search for non-citizens who can be
made subject to deportation under
the emigration laws.


Aside from these government
spice, the city is believed to be
flooded with Pinkerton men-the
worst enemies of the revolutions
lists while trying to conduct an or
derby fight. These men, a number
of whom are declared to have been
recognized by members of the l.
w. w. as the same "Pinks" who
operated in the disorders at Gold
field, New, seem to make a prac-
tice of following such disorders.


1t is to these men that officials
of the l. w. w. charge many of the
wild scenes of damaging disorders
that have attended laboring mems
